bridge_concepts: SNAT/ASMT melatonin biosynthesis (animal-type TPH-first in dinoflagellates, same product)
Melatonin-AFMK-AMK ROS scavenging cascade (10x multiplier per molecule)
Enzyme induction (SOD, CAT, APX, GR) at signaling concentrations

kegg.note: Plant melatonin biosynthesis and enzyme entries VERIFIED. Dinoflagellate/Symbiodiniaceae absent from KEGG — infrastructure gap, not negative evidence.

string.note: Core melatonin biosynthesis interaction 0.994 (very high confidence). IDO1 stress-pathway link supports thermal stress context. Dinoflagellate proteins absent from STRING (infrastructure gap).

key_paper.citation: Roopin M, Yacobi YZ, Levy O (2013). Occurrence, diel patterns, and the influence of melatonin on the photosynthetic performance of cultured Symbiodinium. J Pineal Res.
key_paper.significance: Confirmed melatonin in Symbiodinium (nocturnal peaks). Exogenous melatonin ENHANCED PHOTOPROTECTIVE MECHANISMS (NPQ engagement) — decreased O2 evolution via NPQ upregulation. VALIDATES that melatonin-Symbiodinium photosynthetic axis exists. Study under NORMAL conditions only; thermal stress/bleaching context NOT addressed.
key_paper.implication_for_hypothesis: MECHANISTIC FOOTHOLD: melatonin→photoprotection in Symbiodinium is established. Novel angle = thermal stress + bleaching prevention. MUST CITE.

ros_scavenging.verdict: PLAUSIBLE — cascade and enzyme induction resolve concentration gap

uv_photolysis.verdict: PLAUSIBLE — depth attenuation (5-15m typical habitat) and nocturnal synthesis timing mitigate UV lability

geo_expression.note: Raw RNAseq data for Symbiodiniaceae under 26°C vs 32°C heat stress exists in SRA. TPH/AANAT homolog expression NOT yet analyzed. This is a directly testable prediction.

generator_constraints: Melatonin pathway in dinoflagellates is ANIMAL-type (TPH-first: Trp→5-HTP→serotonin→NAS→melatonin), NOT plant-type (TDC-first). Bridge operates through PRODUCT convergence (same melatonin molecule), not pathway homology.

Direct ROS scavenging at resting endogenous concentrations (~215 nM) covers ~24% of OH flux. Generator MUST emphasize NPQ enhancement + enzyme induction (SOD/CAT/APX/GR) + cascade as PRIMARY mechanisms.
MUST CITE Roopin 2013 (PMID 23496383) — directly shows melatonin enhances photoprotective mechanisms in cultured Symbiodinium.
MUST CITE Antolin 1997 (PMID 9462850) — Gonyaulax rescued from lethal oxidative stress by elevated melatonin (~215 μM under stress).
Novel angle = THERMAL STRESS + BLEACHING INTERVENTION context, not melatonin-photosynthesis connection (Roopin 2013 already established that).
Disjointness: 0 papers on melatonin+coral bleaching; 0 on melatonin+Symbiodiniaceae. PARTIALLY_EXPLORED for melatonin+dinoflagellate (29 papers).
Propose mining PRJNA723630 (Camp et al. 2022 SRA) for TPH/AANAT homolog expression under heat stress as first experimental test.
No known melatonin receptor in dinoflagellates (MT1/MT2 are animal GPCRs). Signaling mechanism is unknown — this is an open question to address.
